title: Oxydectes panduriformis
taxonomy: Oxydectes panduriformis is a species within the family Ingeae, which is a subtribe of the Fabaceae family. The genus Oxydectes is placed within the tribe Ingeae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the pea family. This classification reflects Oxydectes panduriformis's botanical affinity with other legume species. Further classification of Oxydectes panduriformis involves its family and subfamily, which are Fabaceae and Faboideae, respectively. The full classification for Oxydectes panduriformis is Oxydectes panduriformis, Fabaceae, Faboideae, Ingeae, Oxydectes.

intro: Oxydectes panduriformis, also known as the 'woodrose' or 'wood rose', is a type of flowering plant native to Asia, characterized by its unique, fragrant flowers. The woodrose plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and promote relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Oxydectes panduriformis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to exhibit various pharmacological activities.
